comment: Flower — a Korg Toneworks 'Random' style sample-and-hold step filter, the 'Digital Bath' (Deftones) middle-eight sound. A resonant filter whose cutoff jumps to a new random value every step at a set Rate, run as independent L/R sequences for a stereo random filter. Named after Pandora PX3 preset #29. Desktop preview implements the full DSP; pedal C port is staged (audio_nop smoke build).
gid_comment: 0x06 = Modulation category (random filter is a modulation effect, like StChorus).
fxid_comment: 0x01C3 = above the repo's used range (400-444), distinct from microloom (450). Verify uniqueness before flashing.

params.name: Rate
params.comment: Steps per second the random filter cutoff jumps (~0.5..14 Hz).

params.name: Range
params.comment: How wide the random cutoff jumps span, in octaves (0..3.5).

params.name: Manual
params.comment: Center frequency the random sweep is built around (~100..2000 Hz, log).

params.name: Reso
params.comment: Filter resonance. High = glassy whistling peak (the Korg character).

params.name: Smooth
params.comment: Glide between steps. 0 = hard glitchy steps; high = liquid sweeps.

params.name: Mix
params.comment: Dry/wet blend. Digital Bath is mostly wet.

audio_nop_comment: Pedal C port starts as a UI-only smoke build. Full DSP validated on desktop first; see tools/audio_preview/renderers/flower.py.
